articles.title: Jammu & Kashmir School Holiday: J&K Extended Vacation for All Government & Private Schools Until July 22 Due to Heatwave & Weather Advisories - The Sunday Guardian
articles.full_text:
The Jammu and Kashmir Government has officially extended the summer vacation for all government and recognized private schools up to the Higher Secondary level until Wednesday, July 22, 2026.
Jammu & Kashmir School Holiday: The Jammu and Kashmir Government has officially extended the ongoing summer vacation by three days, ordering all government and recognized private schools to remain closed until July 22, 2026. Academic classes are now scheduled to formally resume on Thursday, July 23, 2026.
Here’s everything you need to know about the Jammu & Kashmir schools holiday, affected regions, safety advisory, and more.
The Jammu and Kashmir Government has officially extended the summer vacation for all government and recognized private schools up to the Higher Secondary level until Wednesday, July 22, 2026. Educational institutions are scheduled to reopen and resume normal academic activities on Thursday, July 23, 2026.
The official orders issued by the Directorate of School Education Kashmir (DSEK) and the Directorate of School Education Jammu (DSEJ) apply to:
All public and recognized private schools across the entire Kashmir Division.
All schools located in the Winter Zones of the Jammu Division.
The holiday extension from the original reopening date of July 20 was declared by Education Minister Sakina Itoo due to severe environmental risks:
Severe Heatwave: Intense and climbing summer temperatures across the valley made classroom environments unsafe for students.
Inclement Weather Advisory: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) issued a warning for heavy downpours, which carry a significant risk of flash floods and localized landslides.
Parents, students, and institutional authorities are advised to monitor announcements from the School Education Department for any further changes based on local weather shifts.
The expansion of the holidays became necessary due to an abrupt shift from extreme thermal stress to heavy downpour threats:
Record-Breaking Heatwave: Leading up to the extension, the Kashmir Valley recorded unusually harsh summer weather. Srinagar reached a seasonal high of 35.9°C, creating a stifling, high-risk environment for students inside classrooms.
Severe Torrential Rain Forecast: Concurrently, the India Meteorological Department (IMD) issued a warning for widespread heavy to very heavy rainfall.
Infrastructure Risks: The IMD highlighted that the impending downpours carry serious risks of localized flash floods and landslides, which are predicted to disrupt surface transport, including critical arteries like the Jammu-Srinagar National Highway.
School administrations have issued a few reminders to families ahead of the July 23 reopening:
Holiday Assignments: Students are encouraged to use these final days to complete any academic tasks assigned at the start of the July break.
Ignore Rumours: Education Minister Sakina Itoo explicitly urged parents and students to ignore unverified social media claims hinting at a longer, week-long extension, as any further updates will only come through official department channels.
The 3-day school holiday extension in Jammu and Kashmir underscores the regional government's proactive stance on student safety during extreme environmental conditions.
